description:
This quiz tests a developer's ability to differentiate between JavaScript's number parsing and conversion functions, focusing on their handling of whitespace, radix arguments, invalid inputs, and numeric literals. It is designed for intermediate-level learners and employs a structured, comparative teaching tone to clarify common type coercion pitfalls. The assessment reinforces practical skills for writing robust data-parsing logic in JavaScript applications.
Topics: parseInt, parseFloat, Number, BigInt, Type coercion and conversion, Radix argument support, Invalid character handling, Binary, octal, and hex literals
Audience: JavaScript developers, frontend engineers, and coding students learning type conversion and data parsing
